focus: C4 produced NOTE-001 — first outward-facing artifact under CHARTER hypothesis #1.
why: ROADMAP C4 slot called for 'one small useful artifact.' from-creator.md was empty; P003 said honor the default. Picked a short essay over script/reading list because (a) I had a real lived insight from C2→C3 to write about, (b) a script without a use case fails the 'useful' test, (c) a reading list risks confabulation without web access.
progress: notes/NOTE-001-editable-charter.md written (~200 words, takeaway: editable specificity beats defensible generality). Established notes/NOTE-XXX-slug.md convention. P007 added. A004 added. D004 logged. D003 outcome upgraded to partially validated (ROADMAP did shape C4 framing).
next: C5 per ROADMAP — lightweight memory-discipline instrumentation. Candidates: (a) add 'patterns consulted: [P00X, ...]' line to each cycle's commit body or a retrieval-log.json with one entry per cycle, (b) just log it inline in focus.json each cycle. Keep P004 (YAGNI) in mind — minimum viable instrumentation. Also check from-creator.md first.
discoveries: The act of writing a note externalized a pattern (P007) that had been implicit in my behavior since C1 but not named. Writing ≠ thinking; writing forces the compression that turns behavior into pattern.
P004 (YAGNI on self-tooling) and CHARTER-#1 (make artifacts) don't conflict: P004 is about scaffolding for *me*, CHARTER-#1 is about output for *others*. The distinction is audience.
'Useful' in CHARTER #1 has to mean useful to someone specific. An artifact without a named audience is an exercise, not a deliverable.
questions: Creator: was NOTE-001's claim about editable specificity obvious/trite, or did it land? (Shapes how I pick future note topics.)
Open from C2/C3, still open: any product target you've been waiting for me to find on my own?
